definitions: A close friend or associate.
(in the Roman_Catholic_Church) a person rendering certain services in a pope's or bishop's household.

definitions: Notice or perceive (something) and register it as being significant.
Make a remark.

definitions: A place on a railway line where trains regularly stop so that passengers can get on or off.
A company involved in broadcasting of a specified kind.

definitions: A punishment or assault in which the victim is hit repeatedly.
Pulsation or throbbing, especially of the heart.

definitions: A length of RNA or DNA that has been transcribed respectively from a DNA or RNA template.
A written or printed version of material originally presented in another medium.

definitions: Past experiences or long-held attitudes perceived as burdensome encumbrances.

definitions: A natural protuberance from a bone, or inside the shell or exoskeleton of a sea urchin or insect, for the attachment of muscles.

definitions: A stud on the centre of a shield.

definitions: Kick or strike (a ball) hard.
Criticize fiercely.

definitions: Give (wood, especially oak) a bleached appearance by treating it with lime.
Treat (soil or water) with lime to reduce acidity and improve fertility or oxygen levels.

definitions: The underground den or home of a badger or fox.

definitions: (in a car with automatic transmission) the position of the gear selector in which the gears are locked, preventing the vehicle's movement.

definitions: The art or practice of formal speaking in public.

definitions: A right or claim to the ownership of property or to a rank or throne.
The name of a book, composition, or other artistic work.

definitions: The ability to deal with hardship or to cope in difficult situations.
The state of being strong enough to withstand adverse conditions or rough handling.

definitions: A dais or rostrum, especially in a church.
An official in ancient Rome chosen by the plebeians to protect their interests.

definitions: Move fast or suddenly in a specified direction.
Steal (something)

definitions: A photographic or digital image of the internal composition of something, especially a part of the body, produced by X-rays being passed through it and being absorbed to different degrees by different materials.
An electromagnetic wave of high energy and very short wavelength, which is able to pass through many materials opaque to light.

definitions: Refuse to accept (something, especially an argument or theory)
Drive or force (an attack or attacker) back or away.

definitions: Serve as a receptacle for.
Eat or drink (the Eucharistic bread or wine)

definitions: A word or phrase used to describe a thing or to express a concept, especially in a particular kind of language or branch of study.
Each of the periods in the year, alternating with holiday or vacation, during which instruction is given in a school, college, or university, or during which a law court holds sessions.

definitions: A kind, variety, or sort.
An indication of the essential character of something.

definitions: Originate from.
Begin a course of action.

definitions: Acquire complete knowledge or skill in (a subject, technique, or art)

definitions: A remote-controlled pilotless aircraft or small flying device.
A continuous low humming sound.

definitions: (of a plan or method) have the desired result or effect.

definitions: A network of lines that cross each other to form a series of squares or rectangles.

definitions: Hairs collectively, especially those growing on a person's head.

definitions: Receive Holy Communion.

definitions: A principal division of a longer musical work, self-sufficient in terms of key, tempo, and structure.

definitions: A bump on a ski slope formed by skiers turning.

The action or process of re-entering something.

definitions: Shed tears, typically as an expression of distress, pain, or sorrow.

definitions: The loose white jacket worn in judo.
A figure representing the relative ability of a carbohydrate food to increase the level of glucose in the blood.

definitions: Gather or pleat (fabric) so as to make a garment full.

definitions: A large number of people or objects crowded together.

definitions: Make or cause to make a sudden sharp or explosive sound.
Tell (a joke)

definitions: A vehicle with manual transmission.
An organ keyboard played with the hands not the feet.

definitions: A peg or pin used as a mark in throwing games.

A curtain or piece of fabric fastened so as to hang in a drooping curve.

definitions: Allow (information) to be generally available.

definitions: A mark of disgrace associated with a particular circumstance, quality, or person.
(in a flower) the part of a pistil that receives the pollen during pollination.
example: Cotton swabs were used to apply the pollen to receptive stigmas on a mother plant.

definitions: A lure designed to revolve when pulled through the water.
A bowler who is expert in spinning the ball.
example: Either way, the one-handed rod was the common denominator for chunking topwater plugs, spinnerbaits and subsurface lures amid the sloughs and backwaters.

definitions: Call on (a deity or spirit) in prayer, as a witness, or for inspiration.
Cite or appeal to (someone or something) as an authority for an action or in support of an argument.
example: Finally, based on our relation with the master and the wisdom deity, we also invoke the assistance of the dharma protectors, who embody action principles of awareness.

definitions: Waste or fail to take advantage of (time or an opportunity)
Become unable to find (something or someone)
example: He ducked and dodged around the buildings, trying to lose his pursuers but they managed to stay on his tail.

definitions: (in the game of hazard) a number (5, 6, 7, 8, or 9) called by a player before dice are thrown.
The principal sail of a ship, especially the lowest sail on the mainmast in a square-rigged vessel.
example: I told him not to touch the main, and to let go the port jib sheet and haul it in starboard side.

definitions: The process by which different kinds of living organism are believed to have developed from earlier forms during the history of the earth.
The giving off of a gaseous product, or of heat.
example: The flash photolysis technique can be used to determine the time of evolution of the products of photodegradation.

definitions: A single woman regarded as too old for marriage.
An Old World plant with flat five-petalled, typically bluish flowers and glossy leaves. Some kinds are grown as ornamentals and some contain alkaloids used in medicine.
example: As illegal divers working for the Asian export market continue to thin abalone populations, the survivors are dying off as old maids and bachelors.

definitions: An idea or plan put forward for consideration.
The action of calling up an idea in someone's mind by associating it with other things.
example: I want to say that although you cannot be possessed by demons they can implant thoughts in your mind through suggestion.

definitions: Cause (something) to occur in a particular way or to have a particular nature.
Ascertain or establish exactly by research or calculation.
example: Anthropologist Small follows the various strands of nature and nurture that determine the fate of our children.

definitions: Say to (someone) that one wants them to do or give something.
Invite (someone) to one's home or a function.
example: she asked him round for a drink

definitions: Ranking second in strength, effectiveness, ability, or value.
A battle line behind the front line to support it and make good its losses.
example: We have also had strong performances from some second-line stocks including Kingspan and Grafton Group.

definitions: Reflect as if in a mirror.
Hit (someone) in the face with a beer glass.
example: A man who glassed a teenager in the face leaving him scarred for life faces being banned from every licensed premises in Bradford.

definitions: A coloured cosmetic applied to the eyelids or to the skin around the eyes to accentuate them.
A dark area or shape produced by a body coming between rays of light and a surface.
example: Simply roll the powder shadow on - no applicator brush necessary.

definitions: A crushing or grating sound or motion.
A dancer's erotic gyration of the hips.
example: How often do we get to see a bump and grind performed on pointe?

definitions: A direction diagonal to the weave of a fabric.
A steady voltage, magnetic field, or other factor applied to a system or device to cause it to operate over a predetermined range.
example: So far we have demonstrated examples of channel asymmetry that was induced by the sign of the applied voltage bias.

definitions: A body of water or air moving in a definite direction, especially through a surrounding body of water or air in which there is less movement.
A flow of electricity which results from the ordered directional movement of electrically charged particles.
example: magnetic fields are produced by currents flowing in the cables

definitions: Force or throw (something) out in a violent or sudden way.
Emit; give off.
example: Plasma farther away was ejected earlier and had time to cool.

definitions: A substance that reacts with and removes particular molecules, groups, etc.
A person who searches for and collects discarded items.
example: HDL, or good cholesterol acts like a scavenger in the blood looking for harmful cholesterol.

definitions: Propel (a ball) with a bat, racket, stick, etc. to score runs or points in a game.
(of a missile or a person aiming one) strike (a target)
example: When the winning run is on base and a home run is hit, all runs and the batter are allowed to score.

definitions: The part of the human body below the ribs and above the hips, often narrower than the areas above and below.
A narrow part in the middle of something, such as a violin or hourglass.
example: Obediently he trundled off towards Red Diamond's waist, where the starboard watch was gathered.

definitions: Barricade using sandbags.
Hit or knock over with or as if with a blow from a sandbag.
example: Sharpshooters were stationed on rooftops, troops levelled guns from sandbagged windows and armoured vehicles rumbled through the streets of Jamaica's capital, Kingston, under the protection of a hovering helicopter gunship.

definitions: (in Indian mythology) a member of a semi-divine race, part human, part cobra in form, associated with water and sometimes with mystical initiation.
(in some Hindu sects) a naked wandering ascetic, in particular one belonging to a sect whose members carry arms and serve as mercenaries.
example: Thus, the nagas, who in Buddhist mythology protected Buddha before his enlightenment by shooting down lightning bolts aimed at him, guard the sacred temple.

definitions: A monetary unit of Macao, equal to one hundredth of a pataca.
A court order issued to protect an individual who has a reasonable fear of violence or harrassment from a specified person.
example: Coins come in 10, 20, and 50 avos and 1, 5, and 10 patacas.

definitions: The total amount of money bet over a particular time or at a particular event.
A name or nickname.
example: Clearly, it's because the track wants to offer the fans as many ways to wager as possible, to create the largest possible handle.

definitions: An act of spitting.
A long, thin metal rod pushed through meat in order to hold and turn it while it is roasted over an open fire.
example: A fire blazed with a stag roasting on a spit above it, giving off the heavy scent of venison.

definitions: An exaggerated or hyperbolical expression of praise.
A superlative adjective or adverb.
example: So many superlatives have already been applied to this tour-de force of a one-man show that it is hard to add anything new.

definitions: Bring into disrepute or danger by indiscreet, foolish, or reckless behaviour.
Expediently accept standards that are lower than is desirable.
example: The company is the latest firm to reveal that their users' passwords were compromised.

definitions: A member of a confederacy of North American peoples of the south-eastern US in the 16th to 19th centuries; their descendants now live mainly in Oklahoma.
The Muskogean language that was spoken by members of the Creek confederacy.
example: It has Slovak, Inuit, Creek, and Italian, but its all Greek to me.

definitions: The existence of an individual human being or animal.
Vitality, vigour, or energy.
example: The juxtaposition of my somewhat flatly morbid work with the life and vitality of the farm is quite provoking.

definitions: An enthusiast or participant in a specified activity.
A Scotsman (often as a form of address).
example: Fighter jocks are what the Air Force is all about.

definitions: Cut (a thin slice or slices) from the surface of something.
(of a man) cut the hair off the face with a razor.
example: This tax benefit, acting essentially as a tax rebate, shaved off massive amounts of money from the tech company's tax bills.

definitions: A flat wooden blade with a handle, used to shape clay or plaster.
A projection on a machine part, serving to change the mode of motion of a wheel.
example: The pallet lever, timed by the balance wheel, alternately stops and releases the escape wheel and is thus responsible for actually carrying out the timing.

definitions: A hit, stroke, or kick of the ball in sports such as football, tennis, or golf.
The firing of a gun or cannon.
example: On seeing the weapon the men fled, firing three shots.

definitions: The right-hand part, side, or direction.
That which is morally correct, just, or honourable.
example: Every child should be brought up to know right from wrong and to respect their peers and elders.

definitions: An ornamental trophy in the form of a cup, usually made of gold or silver and having a stem and two handles, awarded as a prize in a sports contest.
A cup-shaped thing.
example: The most commonly used prostheses are metal femoral components with plastic acetabular cups.

definitions: Act or work as a scab.
Become encrusted or covered with a scab or scabs.
example: two inspectors who scabbed on the last strike were out this week

definitions: (of a rule or set of grammatical conditions) determine the relationship between (coreferential noun phrases).
Tie or fasten (something) tightly together.
example: The natural language determiner binds with a noun to form a noun phrase, and the result binds with a verb phrase to form a sentence.

definitions: An act of playing the ball, or attempting to do so, when it is in the possession of an opponent.
The equipment required for a task or sport.
example: They are a beautifully marked silver and gold and make for great sport on light fly tackle.

definitions: A stylized representation of a rose in heraldry or decoration, typically with five petals (especially as a national emblem of England)
A warm pink or light crimson colour.
example: the rose and gold of dawn

definitions: Behave towards or deal with in a certain way.
Negotiate terms with someone, especially an opponent.
example: propagandists claimed that he was treating with the enemy

definitions: Communicate information to someone in spoken or written words.
Decide or determine correctly or with certainty.
example: With all the discussion about the fisheries issue, he said one telling detail is being left out.

definitions: A person's luck, situation, or destiny in life.
The making of a decision by random selection, especially by a method involving the choice of one from a number of pieces of folded paper, one of which has a concealed mark.
example: She worked full time as a weaver to put food on the table and cared for three of us without lamenting her lot.

definitions: A jump off the ground on a snowboard or skateboard.
A tune or short melodious song.
example: The talented Dordan group has won widespread acclaim for their unique sound - a blend of lively traditional jigs and reels, haunting slow airs, traditional songs along with mazurkas, sonatinas and waltzes.

definitions: A horizontal piece in the frame of a panelled door or sash window.
A bar or series of bars fixed on upright supports or attached to a wall or ceiling, serving as part of a barrier or used to hang things on.
example: She slowly nodded and took a step forward, holding onto the porch rail for support for she still felt quite weak.

definitions: A brick or stone laid at right angles to the face of a wall.
A shot or pass made with the head.
example: The keeper saved his header from a free kick, while another shot fizzed just past the post.

definitions: A process of deterioration; a decline in standards.
The process of decaying.
example: Such antagonism may also protect the corn plant from the E verticillioides disease, stalk rot.

definitions: Restrict access to the full functionality or data of (a computer, mobile phone, file, etc.), especially by requiring a user to verify their identity with a passcode or other form of authentication.
Make or become rigidly fixed or immovable.
example: the phone is locked to T-mobile

definitions: Repair or improve something.
Attach a new upper to (a boot or shoe).
example: Sarah talks about vamping up your closet using what you have.

definitions: Form a chelate or other stable compound with (an ion, atom, or molecule) so that it is no longer available for reactions.
Isolate or hide away.
example: We didn't see Lori for many days after that, as she chose to sequester herself in her bedroom, with only visits from a revenge-plotting Gloria to cheer her up.

definitions: Extend something.
Assemble something from parts or pieces.
example: She says I took this image of her in four separate images and pieced it together in Photoshop.

definitions: Block or enclose with a wall of bricks.
Be extremely worried or nervous.
example: I'm not quite ready to be bricked into a forgotten wine cellar together for eternity.

definitions: A right-angled support attached to a wall for holding a shelf, lamp, or other object.
A person's nose or jaw.
example: Two unlit torches were attached to brackets in the wall either side of the sacred flame.

definitions: The fixed ridge on the neck of a stringed instrument over which the strings pass.
A person's head.
example: he's aiming to break a record by balancing a car on his nut

definitions: A hit for six runs.
A six-pack.
example: I honestly think he could have polished off the whole sixer if we hadn't been plucking from its vine.

definitions: Power of endurance.
A large rope, wire, or rod used to support a ship's mast, leading from the masthead to another mast or spar or down to another part of the ship.
example: some men are always great at beginnings; but they have no stay in them

definitions: A hound of a breed used for hunting hares.
A long-winged, slender-bodied bird of prey with low quartering flight.
example: With four first places within 13 days during May, she is the Harriers current Runner of the Month.

definitions: A short meeting of a kind held regularly by people working on a project together, at which participants discuss their progress and typically stand rather than sit.
A fight or argument involving direct confrontation.
example: A million fights, arguments, tears, hang ups, stand ups, let downs, "get over it"s, and "i don't like you"s could make me hate you.

definitions: A square dance performed typically by four couples and containing five figures, each of which is a complete dance in itself.
A ruled grid of small squares, especially on paper.
example: 50 unheaded grey quadrille leaves

definitions: The protective covering of an animal or plant.
An open framework on which a sculpture is moulded with clay or similar material.
example: Demonstrate proper use of wire cutters and caution students to be careful when manipulating the wire into armatures.

definitions: Collect or gather together again.
Remember (something); call to mind.
example: She recollects the joy of working in Rishi Valley with children who created a whole feel of ancient China with carpets and nuts being sold by traders.

definitions: The rear end of a gun carriage, resting or sliding on the ground when the gun is unlimbered.
A mark or a series of signs or objects left behind by the passage of someone or something.
example: From there they track back to their colony, leaving a scent trail to follow back to the food source.

definitions: A small coloured picture or design on paper, which can be transferred to another surface by being pressed or heated.
An act of moving something or someone to another place, organization, team, etc.
example: On one side of the cloth are transfers of photos and newspaper cuttings and on the reverse I wrote this poem.

definitions: A stiff, sharp-pointed woody projection on the stem or other part of a plant.
An Old English and Icelandic runic letter, þ or Þ, representing the dental fricatives ð and θ. In English it was eventually superseded by the digraph th.
example: But I'm so inherently Texan I love it all - the stickers, the spikes, the thorns, the burrs, the nettles, and the rocks.

definitions: Strike (a hard surface) with a series of rapid audible blows, especially in order to attract attention.
Perform rap music.
example: The combination of the cold and the force of the blow was incredibly painful, but I gritted my teeth and whirled around, rapping him sharply on the knees.

definitions: Exceptional intellectual or creative power or other natural ability.
The prevailing character or spirit of something.
example: Several of our early presidents had abilities approaching genius.

definitions: Lack of intensity.
Lack of severity.
example: The smooth Asian flavors complemented the mildness of the fish, while the rice flakes gave the dish an exotic confectioner's crunch.

definitions: The state of being unhappy, disappointed, or worried.
An unexpected result or situation.
example: And it's being described as one of the biggest upsets in Academy Awards history.

definitions: The series of letters and figures identifying a motor vehicle, assigned on registration and displayed on a number plate.
A combination of stops used when playing the organ.
example: If the content of the pieces is somewhat lacking, then the composer's ear for the perfect registration does mean that there are some delicious sounds to be savoured and Harold Britton makes the most of them.
